9 hours ago

Press enter or click to view image in full size

I learned Angular twice.
The first time took me two years. Tutorials, courses, copied code, half-finished projects. Two years, and I still froze in interviews.
The second time took thirty days.
Same brain. Same framework. The only difference was the order.
Nobody tells you this, but Angular is not hard. Angular is badly sequenced. Every course dumps components, modules, RxJS, DI, and routing on you in week one, your brain files it all under “vague”, and eighteen months later you can build a form but cannot explain what happens when you click submit.
The roadmap below is the second attempt, cleaned up and handed to four developers I have mentored since. All four went from “I know some Angular” to clearing product-company interviews. Not because they studied more. Because they studied in the right order, one hour a day, thirty days.
Here is the map. Print it, follow it, do not skip days, and do not let me catch you jumping ahead.
The One Rule That Makes This Work
Before day one, understand why every previous attempt failed:
How courses teach How brains learn
